articles.title: April set to hit dry spell after monthslong dry tilt
articles.author: Kevin Myatt

We are entering what is likely to be a prolonged bone-dry period in Virginia, following what has already been several months of below-normal precipitation that has left the majority of the commonwealth in moderate to severe drought on the latest U.S. Drought Monitor.

articles.title: Know the difference between heat exhaustion and heat stroke
articles.body:
As spring heats up and gives way to summer, it’s important to know the signs of heat exhaustion and heat stroke.

Heat usually peaks between noon-3 p.m. so it’s best to avoid going outdoors during these hours. If you must, try limiting yourself to short bursts. Take breaks and be sure you are well hydrated.

As you hydrate, avoid caffeinated or alcoholic beverages. Be sure to drink water throughout stopping periods, but never drink ice-cold water too fast when you’re overheated. If you are overh

articles.author: Ed Oliver

Alabama’s economy runs on rail.

From the farms of the Tennessee Valley to the industries of Birmingham and the docks at the Port of Mobile, more than 3,000 miles of freight rail knit the state together. That network allows producers in places like Northeast Alabama and along Lake Guntersville to move goods efficiently to global markets, turning crops, timber and raw materials into economic growth.

That system works because it is competitive. And that’s exactly what’s at risk.

articles.title: Opinion | Closing Alabama’s primaries won’t strengthen the GOP; it will weaken accountability
articles.author: David Donaldson
articles.body:
As a native Alabamian from a long line of Republicans, I’m deeply troubled by House Bill 541 and the push to close Alabama’s primaries. I attended the Senate committee hearing on Tuesday and had the privilege to speak. I was struck by the gap between the arguments offered by proponents and what Alabama’s election data actually shows.

Advocates for HB541 insist the primary is merely a private party selection process. The “real election” where all voters get their say is in November, they argue.

articles.title: New Alabama Law Targets Birmingham’s Vacant Properties with Registry, Fees

The legislation creates a vacant property registry aimed at holding property owners accountable and speeding up the process for addressing dilapidated buildings across the city.

Under the law, the registration fee is $250 with a 150% increase per year, capping at $1,000.

articles.title: Alabama lawmakers pass farm utility tax relief bill
articles.author: Staff
articles.body:
The Alabama Legislature on Thursday gave final passage to Senate Bill 159 by Senator Jack Williams, R-Wilmer, and Representative Danny Crawford, R-Athens.

Senate Bill 159 exempts certain agricultural operations from Alabama’s utility gross receipts and utility service use taxes. The bill removes taxes on natural gas and electricity used in commercial aquaculture aeration systems, greenhouses, pivot irrigation systems and poultry houses. The exemption will take effect September 1, 2026.

articles.title: COLUMN: I’ve never been to Oklahoma, but I’ve been to Spain

articles.body:
